news.title: Rupee slips on week as Iran flare-up spurs caution, merchant hedging
news.description: The Indian rupee ended nearly unchanged on Friday, but the week closed on a weaker note. Investor caution grew amid renewed hostilities in the Middle East, impacting oil-affiliated currencies. news.description: Christopher Wood of Jefferies warns that the AI trade is showing signs of fatigue as investors seek cheaper value opportunities. news.title: Global Market: European shares edge higher as miners, airlines offset tech weakness; benchmark set for weekly loss
news.description: European shares saw minimal change as mining and travel stocks gained. Technology shares declined, weighing on the broader market's performance. Investors remained cautious due to renewed Middle East geopolitical tensions. EasyJet shares surged significantly after a takeover approach was revealed.

news.title: Oman Tells UN It’s Against Imposing Transit Fees in Hormuz
news.description: Oman told the United Nations’ shipping agency that it doesn’t support imposing transit fees in the Strait of Hormuz, an approach which might set the sultanate at odds with Iran, which is pushing to charge passing freighters for navigation.

news.title: Polestar's Q2 sales volumes slide amid US market ban
